output.final_deliverable:
1) Exact H1 text from https://example.com: "Example Domain" (source: https://example.com).
2) Exact domain (hostname) of the external link on https://example.com: "iana.org" — the page links to https://iana.org/domains/example (source: https://example.com).
3) URL citations for each visited page: https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved.
output.action_summary: I visited https://example.com and extracted the H1 text "Example Domain" and found an external link to https://iana.org/domains/example (hostname: iana.org). I also visited the IANA reserved-domains page at https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved. Citations: https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved.

b1d20e80-0cb5-450f-9d40-4f1fda81b25a.title:
Visit https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved. Return: (1) the exact H1 text from example.com, (2) the exact domain of the external link from example.com, and (3) one URL citation for each visited page.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

details.mandate:
Visit https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved. Return: (1) the exact H1 text from example.com, (2) the exact domain of the external link from example.com, and (3) one URL citation for each visited page.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

merged_results.title: Plan extraction: target facts, sources, and verification steps

result.title: Plan extraction: target facts, sources, and verification steps

details.text: Plan: 1) Target facts to extract: (A) exact H1 text from https://example.com, (B) exact domain (hostname) of the external link found on example.com, and (C) one URL citation for each visited page (i.e., the visited page URLs themselves). 2) Likely sources: the two provided URLs (https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved). No search required because URLs are given. 3) Verification steps: visit https://example.com and (a) parse the HTML to find the first (or relevant) <h1> and capture its exact inner text (preserving case/spaces), (b) find the external hyperlink(s) on the page, pick the external link referenced in the page content, extract its exact domain/hostname (e.g., example.org → example.org), and (c) record the exact visited URLs as citations. 4) Output fields (measurable): example_h1_text (string), example_external_link_domain (string), example_citation_url (string), iana_citation_url (string). 5) Why this reduces uncertainty: direct visits ensure exact HTML text, exact href values, and authoritative citation URLs. Next actions: visit https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved and extract the specified fields.
details.reason: Documenting exact extraction plan before visiting pages to ensure deterministic parsing and concrete outputs.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

IANA-managed Reserved Domains
Domains
Protocols
Numbers
About
IANA-managed Reserved Domains
Certain domains are set aside, and nominally registered to “IANA”, for specific
		policy or technical purposes.
Example domains
As described in
RFC 2606
and
RFC 6761
,
	a number of domains such as
example.com
and
example.org
are maintained for documentation purposes. These domains may be used as illustrative
	examples in documents without prior coordination with us. They are 
	not available for registration or transfer.
Test IDN top-level domains
These domains were temporarily delegated by IANA for the
IDN Evaluation
being conducted by

Policy-reserved domains
We act as both the registrant and registrar for a select number of domains
		which have been reserved under policy grounds. These exclusions are
		typically indicated in either technical standards (RFC documents),
		or
contractual limitations
.
Domains which are described as registered to IANA or ICANN on policy
		grounds are not available for registration or transfer, with the exception
		of
country-name
.info
domains. These domains are available for release
		by the ICANN Governmental Advisory Committee Secretariat.
Other Special-Use Domains
There is additionally a
Special-Use Domain Names
registry documenting special-use domains designated by technical standards. For further information, see
Special-Use Domain Names
(RFC 6761).

details.text: Plan: 1) Target facts to extract: (A) exact H1 text from https://example.com, (B) exact domain (hostname) of the external link found on example.com, and (C) one URL citation for each visited page (i.e., the visited page URLs themselves). 2) Likely sources: the two provided URLs (https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved). No search required because URLs are given. 3) Verification steps: visit https://example.com and (a) parse the HTML to find the first (or relevant) <h1> and capture its exact inner text (preserving case/spaces), (b) find the external hyperlink(s) on the page, pick the external link referenced in the page content, extract its exact domain/hostname (e.g., example.org → example.org), and (c) record the exact visited URLs as citations. 4) Output fields (measurable): example_h1_text (string), example_external_link_domain (string), example_citation_url (string), iana_citation_url (string). 5) Why this reduces uncertainty: direct visits ensure exact HTML text, exact href values, and authoritative citation URLs. eef9be1b-efb3-40bd-95d7-5fa6073bb872.title: Visit https://example.com and extract H1 text, external link domain, and citation URL

details.intent: Extract exact H1 inner text, find the external link on the page and extract its exact domain/hostname, and return the visited page URL as citation.

output_fields.example_h1_text: exact inner text of the page's H1 element (preserve case and spacing)
output_fields.example_external_link_domain: exact hostname/domain of the external link referenced on the page (e.g., example.org)
output_fields.example_citation_url: the exact visited URL to cite (https://example.com)

details.why: Visiting the provided page yields definitive HTML from which the H1 text and link href can be extracted; this eliminates ambiguity from search snippets.
details.parent_goal:
Visit https://example.com and https://www.iana.org/domains/reserved. Return: (1) the exact H1 text from example.com, (2) the exact domain of the external link from example.com, and (3) one URL citation for each visited page.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.
